Add YouTube Live Chat Bot with toggle and custom messages

- _ChatBot class: OAuth2 auth code flow, live chat polling, keyword triggers,
  random messages on configurable interval, headless message on stream start
- start_bot()/stop_bot() wired into start_stream()/stop_stream()
- GET  /bot_status      — JSON bot state (running, messages_sent, authorized)
- GET  /bot_auth_url    — returns OAuth2 authorization URL
- GET  /oauth2callback  — exchanges code, saves token, shows success page
- POST /bot_config      — save all bot settings (enabled toggle, messages, triggers)
- Chat Bot panel in web UI: enable toggle, OAuth setup (collapsible), headless
  message textarea, random messages list (add/remove), keyword trigger table
  (add/remove), interval picker, live status dot
- Bot disabled by default — safe for users who don't need it

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(controller): add sync jitter and circuit breaker for API resilience (#149)

* Initial plan

* feat(controller): add sync jitter and circuit breaker for API resilience

Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: RecordFailure returns bool to avoid race condition on event emission

Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(circuit-breaker): address review comments - single probe, entry comment, and RecordFailure in all controllers

Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(circuit-breaker): remove noisy events, extract helper, add configurable thresholds

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/joelp172/uptime-robot-operator/sessions/8e507735-e3a5-4f2f-9352-373eda09f772

Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(circuit-breaker): harden circuit breaker reliability and controller integration

Move circuit breaker check after deletion handling in monitor,
maintenancewindow, and slackintegration controllers so finalizer
cleanup is not blocked when the circuit is open. Add missing
onTransientAPIFailure calls to four inline error paths in the
monitor controller (EditMonitor, StartMonitor, PauseMonitor x2)
that previously bypassed the circuit breaker entirely.

Also: validate constructor inputs, immediately reopen on HalfOpen
probe failure regardless of counter, move RecordSuccess before
heartbeat publish, record success after deletion cleanup, add
Remove method for stale entries, deduplicate Open-to-HalfOpen
promotion, add String() to CircuitState, and cover all new
behavior with tests.

* fix(circuit-breaker): add recovery events, handle non-transient errors in HalfOpen

Rename onTransientAPIFailure to onAPIFailure and expand it to also
record failures for non-transient errors when the circuit is HalfOpen,
preventing the probe slot from being permanently blocked. Add onAPISuccess
helper that emits a CircuitBreakerClosed Kubernetes event when the circuit
recovers. Replace all direct DefaultCircuitBreaker.RecordSuccess calls
with onAPISuccess across all four controllers.

* fix(circuit-breaker): use cluster-scoped account key, init metrics, prevent probe slot leak

Account is cluster-scoped so namespace is always empty — use
account.Name directly instead of namespace/name to avoid "/<name>" keys
in metrics and logs. Initialize the circuit breaker Prometheus gauge to
CircuitClosed on first entry creation so the metric series exists even
for healthy accounts. Move onAPISuccess() before Kubernetes status
writes in all controller success paths so the HalfOpen probe slot is
released even if a status update fails.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Joel Pinder <joel.pinder@adarga.ai>

commit.message:
[chore](build) Add code style enforcement documentation, skills, and clang-tidy script (#62307)

Problem Summary:
The AGENTS.md and Copilot skills had no documentation about code style
enforcement workflows for BE (C++ clang-format/clang-tidy) or FE (Java
checkstyle). This made it unclear how and when to run formatting and
static analysis tools before committing code changes.

This PR adds:
1. A "Code Style Enforcement" subsection under "Coding Standards" in
AGENTS.md covering BE clang-format, BE clang-tidy, and FE checkstyle.
2. Three new skills: `be-code-style`, `fe-code-style`,
`clang-tidy-check`, each providing step-by-step procedures for fixing
style issues.
3. A new `build-support/run-clang-tidy.sh` script that runs clang-tidy
incrementally on **only changed lines** (not entire files) by parsing
git diff, avoiding noise from pre-existing code.

### Release note

None

### Check List (For Author)

- Test: Manual test
- Verified check-format.sh, run-clang-tidy.sh (--help, --files, --base,
--full, line-level filtering logic), and FE checkstyle all work
correctly
- Behavior changed: No
- Does this need documentation: No (this IS documentation)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(research): loop-deconfounded therapy estimation (EXP-1881–1888)

8 experiments decomposing AID loop compensation to assess true
therapy parameter accuracy across 11 patients.

Key findings:
- EXP-1881: Loop overwhelmingly REDUCES delivery (8/11 patients)
- EXP-1882: Profile ISF ~2× too high during loop-inactive windows
- EXP-1883: Loop hides 65% of CR error via silent correction
- EXP-1884: Scheduled basal delivered only 4% of the time
- EXP-1885: Overnight drift +2.8 mg/dL/h despite loop management
- EXP-1886: 8/11 basal too high, 7/11 CR too low per loop signals
- EXP-1887: Raw params more stable (9% vs 26%) — loop masks drift
- EXP-1888: Mean 2.5/3 therapy params wrong per patient

Core insight: AID loops are so effective at compensation that they
render therapy settings unobservable. Settings are wrong for nearly
every patient, but outcomes are acceptable because the loop
continuously corrects. The 'oscillation paradox' — scheduled basal
is too high (loop suspends) but effective rate is too low (glucose
rises) — is a fundamental limitation of incorrect settings under
AID management.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Build offline mode test suite with sync conflict scenarios (#371)

Created comprehensive test suite for offline-first behavior and sync
conflict resolution using Last-Write-Wins strategy:

OfflineModeTest (8 test cases):
- Local writes succeed when completely offline
- Multiple sets logged offline persist to local database
- Sync operations queued when offline
- Offline to online transition triggers sync queue flush
- Data integrity maintained through offline-online-offline cycles
- Intermittent connectivity does not block local writes
- Workout completion persists offline
- Network timeout during sync does not affect local data

SyncConflictTest (8 test cases):
- Last write wins when local modification is newer
- Last write wins when remote modification is newer
- Concurrent edits to different fields merge correctly
- Identical timestamps favor local version
- Sync interruption preserves partial progress
- Resume sync after interruption skips already-synced entities
- Deleted entity locally propagates to cloud on sync
- Conflicting delete and edit resolves to delete

All tests use MockK + kotlinx.coroutines.test (runTest) following
existing repository test patterns. Tests verify offline-first data
persistence, network state transitions, and conflict resolution logic.

Closes #333

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix repository failure tests: correct mock patterns and retry verification

Changed Flow error mocking from flowOf().also { throw } to flow { throw }:
- flowOf().also { throw } throws during mock setup, not during collection
- flow { throw } throws during collection, allowing .catch { } to fire

All retry expectations match actual retryWithBackoff implementation:
- Retryable (3x): SQLiteDatabaseLockedException, SQLiteDiskIOException, IOException
- Non-retryable (1x): RuntimeException, IllegalStateException, IllegalArgumentException

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix force-approval spam, conflict warning dedup, TE coverage signal, PE worker status

Bug fixes:
- Force-approval: each reviewer agent now checks if it's actually a required
  reviewer for the PR before posting a force-approval comment. Previously all
  3 agents (PE, PM, Architect) would post redundant force-approvals even when
  only 2 were needed for merge.
- Conflict warnings: check existing PR comments before posting duplicate
  conflict detection warnings. PR #317 had 6 identical warnings from 6 commits.
- TE coverage signal: added CheckTestCoverageCompleteAsync() that sets status
  to 'coverage met, tests complete' when all merged code PRs are tested. The
  HealthMonitor picks this up to emit testing.coverage.met, unblocking the
  Testing→Review phase transition. Previously this signal was NEVER emitted.
- PE worker status: worker PEs now show 'Working on tasks' instead of the
  misleading 'Orchestrating tasks' which is leader-only behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: DevTeam
repository.description: An agent based dev team of a PM, architect, principal engineer, research and senior and junior engineers.

commit.message:
Prominent plateau alert banner on Progress screen (#370)

* Enhance plateau alert cards with severity levels and coaching CTA

- Add PlateauSeverity enum (MILD, MODERATE, SEVERE) based on weeks duration
- Calculate severity: 3-4 weeks = mild, 5-6 = moderate, 7+ = severe
- Add daysSinceLastPR field to PlateauAlert model
- Implement color coding: amber for mild, orange for moderate, red for severe
- Add "Get Coaching Advice" CTA button that navigates to AI Coach with pre-filled prompt
- Add NavigateToCoach effect with prompt parameter in ProgressContract
- Sort plateau alerts by severity (severe first) then by weeks duration
- Add empty state when no plateaus detected: "No plateaus detected — keep crushing it!"
- Show days since last PR alongside weeks stalled in alert details
- Update Spanish translations for new strings
- Wire coaching navigation through savedStateHandle to CoachChatRoute

Closes #363

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update Neo history with plateau alert learnings

fix: correct 3 method descriptions in therapy-tbr-safety report

- Safety tiers: TBR-only thresholds for Low/Moderate/High, not compound
  OR logic with severe episodes (compound only for Critical tier)
- Safety-first protocol: cascading elif (first match wins), not additive
  IF/IF/IF. Stacking alert not implemented in v10 pipeline
- Patient g downgrade: caused by overcorrection rate (39.7%), not
  'nocturnal stacking' which is not a factor in v10 score formula

All per-patient table data verified correct against JSON.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Task 5: Vector search engine (TF-IDF + cosine similarity)

- Builds TF-IDF matrix from crawled Wikipedia pages using scikit-learn
- Ranks documents by cosine similarity to free-text queries
- Supports interactive and single-query CLI modes
- Uses sublinear TF, L2 normalization, English stop words
- Shows top-K results with scores and URLs
